_comment: Mount registry — the audio namespace and its domain ownership, per docs/PLATFORM_V2_ARCHITECTURE.md (Tier 2). All audio publishes to the rack Icecast (distribution LXC) as a source client named here; non-audio artifacts are served by the owning compute UI, never as mounts. NPMplus routing and the compute UIs agree on this table. Nothing parses this yet — humans and later phases do.

icecast.note: icecast.rg2.io still proxies to the Pi (V1). It repoints to the distribution LXC at cutover — see the runbook in docs/deployment_notes.md.

/fm.mp3.source: primary FM channel (back-compat URL preserved from V1)

/fm-<freq>.mp3.source: additional FM channels from the multistation mux (<=4 total)

/scanner-atc.mp3.source: ATC audio (when the R2 is on ATC)

legacy_v1_mounts._comment: V1 mounts and their migration state. 2026-06-10 scanner cutover: /ems.mp3 is RACK-SOURCED (op25 on scanner-compute -> rack Icecast; the Pi Icecast carries an on-demand relay so the public URL keeps working until the NPMplus repoint). The category sub-mounts and monitor are DARK in the interim — the single op25 stream carries all talkgroups (as V1's /ems.mp3 did); per-category splits and the monitor/manual-tune path return as scanner v2 app work once the Airspy R2 lands.

/fm.mp3.source: rx_fm+redsea V1-parity chain (256k, radio-compute, remote dx-R2)

/monitor.mp3.source: scanner-transcribe monitor loop (needs the dongle the remote source now owns)
